title: Trofie al pesto con fagiolini e patate
subtitle: Trofie with pesto, green beans, and potato
excerpt: Fusilli or trofie tossed with fresh pesto, boiled green beans, and small potato cubes, all cooked together in the same pot. The full Ligurian treatment of pesto pasta, where the vegetables are not an addition but part of the dish from the start.
sourceNotes: Based on the Ligurian trofie al pesto con fagiolini e patate tradition as documented in Marcella Hazan, 'Essentials of Classic Italian Cooking' (1992) and Giorgio Locatelli, 'Made in Italy' (2006). Trofie is a short, hand-rolled Ligurian pasta; fusilli is an accessible substitute with a similar sauce-catching shape.

content.text: Trofie al pesto with green beans and potato is the complete version of the Ligurian pesto dish, three things sharing the same pot of water, timed so they are all ready simultaneously and tossed together with the sauce. The potato cubes boil until just tender, the green beans go in partway through, and the pasta finishes the timing. Everything drains together. This is not a recipe from restaurant kitchens; it is exactly what Ligurian home cooks eat.

Trofie is a short, hand-twisted pasta from Recco and the Ligurian coast. Springy and uneven, it has enough surface to grip the pesto. Fusilli is the best substitute in British supermarkets. Same short, twisted shape, just as good at holding a thick sauce.

items.name: Fusilli
items.prepNote: or trofie if available; fusilli is the practical substitute

content.text: Pound {{garlic}} garlic with a pinch of salt in a mortar. Add {{pine-nuts}} of pine nuts and pound until broken. Add {{basil}} basil leaves in batches and pound to a rough paste. Stir in {{parmesan}} parmesan, {{pecorino}} pecorino, and {{olive-oil}} of olive oil. Set aside.

Bring a large pan of water to the boil and season with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt. Add {{potato}} of potato cubes and cook for 5 minutes. Add {{fusilli}} of fusilli and cook for the time on the packet minus 2 minutes. Add {{green-beans}} of green beans and cook for the remaining 2 minutes. Check that the pasta, potato, and beans are all tender simultaneously. Reserve a large mug of pasta water, then drain everything together.

Transfer the drained pasta and vegetables to a large bowl. Mix 4 tablespoons of hot pasta water into the pesto to loosen, then pour over the pasta. Toss gently until everything is coated, adding more pasta water if needed. Serve immediately.

items.fix: Use a waxy potato (Charlotte, Nicola, or similar). Add them first and only cook until just tender, a knife should meet slight resistance.
items.cause: Floury rather than waxy potato, or overcooked

items.fix: Make the pesto while the pasta boils and dress the pasta immediately off the heat.
items.cause: Pesto heated or made too far in advance

content.text: The full trofie dish (pasta, potato, and green beans all boiled together and dressed with pesto) is uniquely Ligurian. The potato and beans are not a garnish but the traditional method. The potato soaks up some of the pesto and gives a soft contrast to the pasta. The beans add freshness. This is the version described in the oldest Ligurian recipes, and the one Ligurian grandmothers still make.
